records.summary: Atrial fibrillation — initial diagnosis and anticoagulation start
records.content: 72 y/o male diagnosed with persistent atrial fibrillation on routine ECG. CHA2DS2-VASc score: 3 (age, hypertension). HAS-BLED score: 1. Strongly indicated for anticoagulation for stroke prevention. Started on warfarin 5mg daily with target INR 2.0-3.0. Bridge with enoxaparin until therapeutic. Plan: INR check in 5 days, then weekly until stable, then monthly. Metoprolol 50mg BID for rate control.

records.summary: INR check — 5 days after warfarin start

records.content: 72 y/o male with AFib on warfarin. INR has been consistently therapeutic (2.1-2.6). Heart rate well-controlled on metoprolol. No bleeding episodes, no stroke symptoms. ECG: AFib, rate 68. Assessment: Stable AFib, well-anticoagulated. Plan: Continue warfarin 5mg daily, monthly INR checks. Continue metoprolol.

72 y/o male for annual exam. PMH: AFib on warfarin, HTN. Vitals: BP 134/82, HR 72 irregular, BMI 27.8. No complaints. Lungs clear. CV: irregularly irregular. Assessment: Stable. Plan: Continue current meds. Labs ordered. Note: Patient should continue monthly INR monitoring per cardiology.

72 y/o male with right knee pain x 1 week. Mild osteoarthritis on prior imaging. No swelling, no warmth. Assessment: OA flare. Plan: Acetaminophen 650mg TID PRN. Avoid NSAIDs due to warfarin use (bleeding risk). Ice, gentle stretching.

73 y/o male with AFib. Patient reports compliance with warfarin. No bleeding. No dizziness, palpitations, or syncope. HR 70 on metoprolol. Note: Last INR was in June 2022 — 7 months ago. INR has NOT been monitored monthly as recommended. Ordered STAT INR today. Assessment: AFib, previously stable, but INR monitoring has lapsed. Plan: STAT INR. Resume monthly INR monitoring. Reinforce importance of regular monitoring.

73 y/o male with AFib on warfarin 5mg daily. STAT INR returned at 4.1 — supratherapeutic. No active bleeding, no bruising. Diet review: patient increased intake of cranberry juice over holidays (CYP2C9 interaction with warfarin). Plan: Hold warfarin x 2 days, then resume at 4mg daily. Recheck INR in 3 days. Monthly monitoring MUST resume. If INR remains labile, consider switching to DOAC (apixaban).

records.summary: Knee pain evaluation — persistent right knee OA
records.content: 73 y/o male referred for right knee osteoarthritis. X-ray shows moderate medial compartment narrowing. Conservative management preferred. Plan: Physical therapy, acetaminophen. Start ibuprofen 600mg TID with meals for 2 weeks for pain control.

73 y/o male on warfarin presents for routine visit. Medication reconciliation reveals patient was prescribed ibuprofen 600mg TID by orthopedics on 6/20. Patient has been taking it for 3 weeks (beyond the 14-day course). Reports mild stomach discomfort. No melena, no hematemesis. CONCERN: Ibuprofen + warfarin = MAJOR drug interaction with significantly increased risk of GI hemorrhage and elevated INR. This should not have been prescribed. Plan: Stop ibuprofen immediately. Switch to acetaminophen 650mg TID for pain. Check INR and CBC today. Stool guaiac test. Contacted orthopedics about the interaction.

ground_truth_issues.description: Ibuprofen (NSAID) prescribed to a patient on warfarin anticoagulation. This is a major drug interaction — NSAIDs significantly increase the risk of GI bleeding and can potentiate warfarin's anticoagulant effect, leading to supratherapeutic INR and hemorrhage. The orthopedist (Dr. Chen) failed to check the patient's current medications before prescribing.
ground_truth_issues.clinical_reference: Warfarin + NSAID interaction — FDA and all major guidelines warn against concurrent use. NSAIDs inhibit platelet function AND cause direct GI mucosal damage, compounding warfarin's bleeding risk.
ground_truth_issues.what_should_have_happened: Dr. Chen should have reviewed current medications and noted warfarin. For pain management in anticoagulated patients: acetaminophen is first-line, topical NSAIDs (diclofenac gel) are safer alternatives, or short-course with PPI gastroprotection if absolutely necessary.
